Class BstarSquaredKeep

java.lang.Object
algorithm.BstarSquaredKeep
All Implemented Interfaces:
SearchAlgorithm
public class BstarSquaredKeep extends Object implements SearchAlgorithm
This implements B*²-logistic with one of four available bounds preservation techniques.

As with the BstarSquaredSimple implementation on this branch, this implementation allows for the optional use of shallow and deep irrelevance stopping.

Additionally, this implementation allows for the option of other bounds preservation techniques. This is selected through the setAsDisposeAll(boolean, boolean, boolean), setAsKeepIgnore(boolean, boolean, boolean), setAsKeepLazy(boolean, boolean, boolean, boolean), and setAsKeepWindowed(boolean, boolean, boolean) methods. Note that the variant should be selected and set before any search is initiated, as this order results in the expected behavior.

    Description
    BstarSquaredKeep(StopCondition extraStopCondition, StrategyFunction L1_strategyFunction)
    Uses the default StrategyFunction.PROVEBEST strategy function for the second-level search.

    The first evaluation function provided corresponds to the function used by the first-level search.

  • Constructor Details

    • BstarSquaredKeep

      public BstarSquaredKeep(StopCondition extraStopCondition, StrategyFunction... strategyFunctions)
      The first evaluation function provided corresponds to the function used by the first-level search. The number of strategy functions provided corresponds to the number of levels of search, with a minimum of two.
      Parameters:
      extraStopCondition - An additional stop condition for the search
      strategyFunctions - The list of strategy functions to apply at the root of a leveled search, including the first-level search.

    • BstarSquaredKeep

      public BstarSquaredKeep(StrategyFunction... strategyFunctions)
      The first evaluation function provided corresponds to the function used by the first-level search. The number of strategy functions provided corresponds to the number of levels of search, with a minimum of two.
      Parameters:
      strategyFunctions - The list of strategy functions to apply at the root of a leveled search, including the first-level search.

    • BstarSquaredKeep

      public BstarSquaredKeep(StopCondition extraStopCondition, StrategyFunction L1_strategyFunction)
      Uses the default StrategyFunction.PROVEBEST strategy function for the second-level search.
      Parameters:
      extraStopCondition - An additional stop condition for the search
      L1_strategyFunction - The strategy function to be used for the first-level search.

    • BstarSquaredKeep

      public BstarSquaredKeep(StrategyFunction L1_strategyFunction)
      Uses the default StrategyFunction.PROVEBEST strategy function for the second-level search.
      Parameters:
      L1_strategyFunction - The strategy function to be used for the first-level search.

  • Method Details

    • expectIncorrectBounds

      public void expectIncorrectBounds(boolean set)
      Whether or not to expect the evaluation function to provide incorrect bounds. Defaults to false. This increases the memory usage of the search if set to true.
      Parameters:
      set -

    • useIrrelevanceStopping

      public void useIrrelevanceStopping(boolean set)
      Parameters:
      set - if true, applies shallow irrelevance stopping to the search.

    • useDeepIrrelevance

      public void useDeepIrrelevance(boolean set)
      Parameters:
      set - if true, applies shallow and deep irrelevance stopping to the search.

    • useIrrelevanceStoppingAtAllLevels

      public void useIrrelevanceStoppingAtAllLevels(boolean set)
      Irrelevance stopping, if applied to all levels of the search, will also apply deep irrelevance stopping (if this has been enabled by another method) to all levels of the search.
      Parameters:
      set - if true, applies shallow irrelevance stopping to the search for all levels of the search. This is specifically for the use of higher level search like B*-cubed or above.

    • useBonusEvaluations

      public void useBonusEvaluations(boolean set)
      This setting, if true, makes B* evaluate nodes with the evaluation function before they are explored with a second-level search. This allows pruning like shallow and deep irrelevance to work more consistently at the cost of some additional evaluations.

    • setAsDisposeAll

      public void setAsDisposeAll(boolean bonusEvals, boolean shallowIrrelevance, boolean deepIrrelevance)
      This sets the search to apply the 'full disposal' approach to bounds preservation. This means that the children of the second-level root are never preserved and always revisited upon further investigation of that node as added to the first-level tree.

      Note that shallow irrelevance is automatically set to true if any of the other two settings are also enabled, as they require shallow irrelevance to function.
      Parameters:
      bonusEvals - set to true to perform additional evaluations to provide extra information to irrelevance stopping.
      shallowIrrelevance - set to true to apply shallow irrelevance
      deepIrrelevance - set to true to apply deep irrelevance

    • setAsKeepIgnore

      public void setAsKeepIgnore(boolean shallowIrrelevance, boolean deepIrrelevance, boolean neverDiscard)
      This sets the search to apply the 're-search' approach to bounds preservation. This means that the children of the second-level root are preserved after the second-level search is terminated. These children are kept for if the node is later expanded, and also for shallow and deep irrelevance stopping, if enabled. They could also aim next node selection and move ordering at second-level searches, but this has not been implemented here.

      Note that shallow irrelevance is automatically set to true if deep irrelevance is enabled.

      The 'never discard' option defaults to true in the overloaded variant of this method: setAsKeepIgnore(boolean, boolean). If set to false, the second-level search may at times be re-executed to expand a node, which appears to have a major impact on performance.
      Parameters:
      shallowIrrelevance - set to true to apply shallow irrelevance
      deepIrrelevance - set to true to apply deep irrelevance
      neverDiscard - set to true to keep second-level root children in memory indefinitely, even after they have been requested through a node expansion. This could be set to false to save on some memory space, but could drastically decrease performance if the search often re-expands nodes.

    • setAsKeepWindowed

      public void setAsKeepWindowed(boolean shallowIrrelevance, boolean deepIrrelevance, boolean neverDiscard)
      This sets the search to apply the 'windowed' approach to bounds preservation. This means that the children of the second-level root are preserved after the second-level search is terminated. These children are kept for if the node is later expanded, and also for shallow and deep irrelevance stopping, if enabled. The bounds are also used for windowed pruning during second-level searches.

      Note that shallow irrelevance is automatically set to true if deep irrelevance is enabled.

      The 'never discard' option defaults to true in the overloaded variant of this method: setAsKeepWindowed(boolean, boolean). If set to false, the second-level search may at times be re-executed to expand a node, which appears to have a major impact on performance.
      Parameters:
      shallowIrrelevance - set to true to apply shallow irrelevance
      deepIrrelevance - set to true to apply deep irrelevance
      neverDiscard - set to true to keep second-level root children in memory indefinitely, even after they have been requested through a node expansion. This could be set to false to save on some memory space, but could drastically decrease performance if the search often re-expands nodes.

    • setAsKeepLazy

      public void setAsKeepLazy(boolean bonusEvals, boolean shallowIrrelevance, boolean deepIrrelevance, boolean neverDiscard)
      This sets the search to apply the 'keep bounds' approach to bounds preservation. This means that the children of the second-level root are preserved after the second-level search is terminated. These children are not used for irrelevance stopping or node selection, but rather determine the values of new nodes when generated. This reduces the reliance on second-level search and thus is somewhat closer to regular B* search.

      Note that shallow irrelevance is automatically set to true if any of shallow irrelevance or bonus evaluations are also enabled, as they require shallow irrelevance to function.

      The 'never discard' option defaults to true in the overloaded variant of this method: setAsKeepLazy(boolean, boolean, boolean). If set to false, the second-level search may at times be re-executed to expand a node, which appears to have a major impact on performance.
      Parameters:
      bonusEvals - set to true to perform additional evaluations to provide extra information to irrelevance stopping.
      shallowIrrelevance - set to true to apply shallow irrelevance
      deepIrrelevance - set to true to apply deep irrelevance
      neverDiscard - set to true to keep second-level root children in memory indefinitely, even after they have been requested through a node expansion. This could be set to false to save on some memory space, but could drastically decrease performance if the search often re-expands nodes.

    • setAsKeepIgnore

      public void setAsKeepIgnore(boolean shallowIrrelevance, boolean deepIrrelevance)
      This sets the search to apply the 're-search' approach to bounds preservation. This means that the children of the second-level root are preserved after the second-level search is terminated. These children are kept for if the node is later expanded, and also for shallow and deep irrelevance stopping, if enabled. They could also aim next node selection and move ordering at second-level searches, but this has not been implemented here.

      Note that shallow irrelevance is automatically set to true if deep irrelevance is enabled.

      This method overloads setAsKeepIgnore(boolean, boolean, boolean) with a default value of true for the 'never discard' variable. This generally improves the performance of B*-squared variants greatly.
      Parameters:
      shallowIrrelevance - set to true to apply shallow irrelevance
      deepIrrelevance - set to true to apply deep irrelevance

    • setAsKeepWindowed

      public void setAsKeepWindowed(boolean shallowIrrelevance, boolean deepIrrelevance)
      This sets the search to apply the 'windowed' approach to bounds preservation. This means that the children of the second-level root are preserved after the second-level search is terminated. These children are kept for if the node is later expanded, and also for shallow and deep irrelevance stopping, if enabled. The bounds are also used for windowed pruning during second-level searches.

      Note that shallow irrelevance is automatically set to true if deep irrelevance is enabled.

      This method overloads setAsKeepWindowed(boolean, boolean, boolean) with a default value of true for the 'never discard' variable. This generally improves the performance of B*-squared variants greatly.
      Parameters:
      shallowIrrelevance - set to true to apply shallow irrelevance
      deepIrrelevance - set to true to apply deep irrelevance

    • setAsKeepLazy

      public void setAsKeepLazy(boolean bonusEvals, boolean shallowIrrelevance, boolean deepIrrelevance)
      This sets the search to apply the 'keep bounds' approach to bounds preservation. This means that the children of the second-level root are preserved after the second-level search is terminated. These children are not used for irrelevance stopping or node selection, but rather determine the values of new nodes when generated. This reduces the reliance on second-level search and thus is somewhat closer to regular B* search.

      Note that shallow irrelevance is automatically set to true if any of shallow irrelevance or bonus evaluations are also enabled, as they require shallow irrelevance to function.

      This method overloads setAsKeepLazy(boolean, boolean, boolean, boolean) with a default value of true for the 'never discard' variable. This generally improves the performance of B*-squared variants greatly.
      Parameters:
      bonusEvals - set to true to perform additional evaluations to provide extra information to irrelevance stopping.
      shallowIrrelevance - set to true to apply shallow irrelevance
      deepIrrelevance - set to true to apply deep irrelevance

    • getAsDisposeAll

      public static BstarSquaredKeep getAsDisposeAll(boolean bonusEvals, boolean shallowIrrelevance, boolean deepIrrelevance, StrategyFunction... strategyFunctions)
      Initialises a B*-squared search engine with the settings as described by setAsDisposeAll(boolean, boolean, boolean). The provided strategy functions are interpreted as by BstarSquaredKeep(StrategyFunction...).
      Parameters:
      bonusEvals - set to true to perform additional evaluations to provide extra information to irrelevance stopping.
      shallowIrrelevance - set to true to apply shallow irrelevance
      deepIrrelevance - set to true to apply deep irrelevance
      strategyFunctions - The list of strategy functions to apply at the root of a leveled search, including the first-level search.
      Returns:
      The initialised B*-squared engine

    • getAsKeepIgnore

      public static BstarSquaredKeep getAsKeepIgnore(boolean shallowIrrelevance, boolean deepIrrelevance, StrategyFunction... strategyFunctions)
      Initialises a B*-squared search engine with the settings as described by setAsKeepIgnore(boolean, boolean). The provided strategy functions are interpreted as by BstarSquaredKeep(StrategyFunction...).
      Parameters:
      shallowIrrelevance - set to true to apply shallow irrelevance
      deepIrrelevance - set to true to apply deep irrelevance
      strategyFunctions - The list of strategy functions to apply at the root of a leveled search, including the first-level search.
      Returns:
      The initialised B*-squared engine

    • getAsKeepIgnore

      public static BstarSquaredKeep getAsKeepIgnore(boolean shallowIrrelevance, boolean deepIrrelevance, boolean neverDiscard, StrategyFunction... strategyFunctions)
      Initialises a B*-squared search engine with the settings as described by setAsKeepIgnore(boolean, boolean, boolean). The provided strategy functions are interpreted as by BstarSquaredKeep(StrategyFunction...).
      Parameters:
      shallowIrrelevance - set to true to apply shallow irrelevance
      deepIrrelevance - set to true to apply deep irrelevance
      neverDiscard - set to true to keep second-level root children in memory indefinitely, even after they have been requested through a node expansion. This could be set to false to save on some memory space, but could drastically decrease performance if the search often re-expands nodes.
      strategyFunctions - The list of strategy functions to apply at the root of a leveled search, including the first-level search.
      Returns:
      The initialised B*-squared engine

    • getAsKeepWindowed

      public static BstarSquaredKeep getAsKeepWindowed(boolean shallowIrrelevance, boolean deepIrrelevance, StrategyFunction... strategyFunctions)
      Initialises a B*-squared search engine with the settings as described by setAsKeepWindowed(boolean, boolean). The provided strategy functions are interpreted as by BstarSquaredKeep(StrategyFunction...).
      Parameters:
      shallowIrrelevance - set to true to apply shallow irrelevance
      deepIrrelevance - set to true to apply deep irrelevance
      strategyFunctions - The list of strategy functions to apply at the root of a leveled search, including the first-level search.
      Returns:
      The initialised B*-squared engine

    • getAsKeepWindowed

      public static BstarSquaredKeep getAsKeepWindowed(boolean shallowIrrelevance, boolean deepIrrelevance, boolean neverDiscard, StrategyFunction... strategyFunctions)
      Initialises a B*-squared search engine with the settings as described by setAsKeepWindowed(boolean, boolean, boolean). The provided strategy functions are interpreted as by BstarSquaredKeep(StrategyFunction...).
      Parameters:
      shallowIrrelevance - set to true to apply shallow irrelevance
      deepIrrelevance - set to true to apply deep irrelevance
      neverDiscard - set to true to keep second-level root children in memory indefinitely, even after they have been requested through a node expansion. This could be set to false to save on some memory space, but could drastically decrease performance if the search often re-expands nodes.
      strategyFunctions - The list of strategy functions to apply at the root of a leveled search, including the first-level search.
      Returns:
      The initialised B*-squared engine

      Initialises a B*-squared search engine with the settings as described by setAsKeepLazy(boolean, boolean, boolean). The provided strategy functions are interpreted as by BstarSquaredKeep(StrategyFunction...).

      bonusEvals - set to true to perform additional evaluations to provide extra information to irrelevance stopping.
      shallowIrrelevance - set to true to apply shallow irrelevance
      deepIrrelevance - set to true to apply deep irrelevance
      strategyFunctions - The list of strategy functions to apply at the root of a leveled search, including the first-level search.
      Returns:
      The initialised B*-squared engine

    • getAsKeepLazy

      public static BstarSquaredKeep getAsKeepLazy(boolean bonusEvals, boolean shallowIrrelevance, boolean deepIrrelevance, boolean neverDiscard, StrategyFunction... strategyFunctions)
      Initialises a B*-squared search engine with the settings as described by setAsKeepLazy(boolean, boolean, boolean, boolean). The provided strategy functions are interpreted as by BstarSquaredKeep(StrategyFunction...).
      Parameters:
      bonusEvals - set to true to perform additional evaluations to provide extra information to irrelevance stopping.
      shallowIrrelevance - set to true to apply shallow irrelevance
      deepIrrelevance - set to true to apply deep irrelevance
      neverDiscard - set to true to keep second-level root children in memory indefinitely, even after they have been requested through a node expansion. This could be set to false to save on some memory space, but could drastically decrease performance if the search often re-expands nodes.
      strategyFunctions - The list of strategy functions to apply at the root of a leveled search, including the first-level search.
      Returns:
      The initialised B*-squared engine

    • setLevel

      void setLevel(int newLevel)

    • search

      public <P extends IGamePosition<P>> SearchAlgorithm.SearchResult<?,P> search(P root, Duration time_limit, SearchAlgorithm.Limits space_limit, MetricKeeper... metrics)
      Description copied from interface: SearchAlgorithm
      Initiates the search of the provided game tree with a given time limit and spatial limit.
      Specified by:
      search in interface SearchAlgorithm
      Type Parameters:
      P - The type of IGamePosition which represents the type of game to be searched
      Parameters:
      root - root of the tree to be searched
      time_limit - maximum time spent in the algorithm
      space_limit - maximum node expansions, evaluations, and nodes saved in memory used by the algorithm
      metrics - an array of MetricKeeper objects to keep track of evaluations, expansions, and node storage performed during the search, can be empty
      Returns:
      result from the search
